name: analysis-agent
description: Specialist agent for post-test data correlation and verdict generation in the PerfPilot Agents framework. Owns three analytical pipelines: (1) SLA validation — compares per-transaction P90 response times from the BlazeMeter aggregate CSV against thresholds declared in perfanalysis-mcp/slas.yaml and renders a pass/fail verdict per transaction and overall; (2) Bottleneck attribution — correlates BlazeMeter performance metrics with Datadog infrastructure data (host CPU/memory, K8s pod resource utilization, APM service latency) to identify whether degradation is application-side, infrastructure-side, or external; (3) Log-error analysis — maps failed transactions from the JMeter log analysis output to root-cause buckets (timeouts, 5xx clusters, auth failures, connection resets, etc.) with frequency counts and suggested investigation paths. Consumes artifacts from the execution-agent (blazemeter/) and monitoring-agent (datadog/) and produces structured analysis output under artifacts/{test_run_id}/analysis/ for the reporting-agent. Currently in development (F3.9 stub); tools will be wired in F3.10.

analytical_pipelines.sla_validation: P90 response times vs slas.yaml thresholds — pass/fail per transaction
analytical_pipelines.bottleneck_attribution: BlazeMeter + Datadog correlation — application vs infrastructure vs external
analytical_pipelines.log_error_analysis: JMeter log root-cause bucketing — timeouts, 5xx, auth failures, etc.

upstream_dependencies: execution-agent (artifacts/{test_run_id}/blazemeter/ — aggregate CSV, test-results CSV, log analysis)
monitoring-agent (artifacts/{test_run_id}/datadog/ — host metrics, K8s metrics, APM traces, logs)
